original text:
In order to build a corporate culture and forge bonds with your partners and customers, we always believe that people need to be part of a common event.
The success of any event organized in France lies in the commitment to running the event.
The outcome of an event will be successful if the event planner inculcates professionalism and expertise skills. The event planner is expected to have a sense of responsibility.
Thus he should use a distinctive, innovative approach that strives to add value to the event.
The budget is an overall determinant of the event quality.
1. WНАТ ТYРЕ ОF ЕVЕΝТ?
An event planner can't organize an event successfully without knowing the type of event.
The event planner needs to know if the corporate event is a meeting, a conference, a business fair or a product launch. It could equally be an alumni event or a recruitment fair.
Whatever the case, knowing the exact nature of the event will enable the event planner to automate manual tasks. Such knowledge will also help to estimate the budget thereby facilitating preparations across the various departments.
2. WНАТ RЕGІОΝЅ НАЅ YОUR РRЕFЕRЕΝСЕ?
If you want to host an event in France, it is also essential to know the right region.
Paris and Riviera-Monaco are some of the best destinations to host events in France. The corporate event planner or French DMC still needs to make a decision on where the event will take place. He may decide that the event will hold at the heart of a prestigious hotel, in a boat or even on the roof of a restaurant.
You should take note that France and most of its big cities have unique event sites. However, an exceptional opportunity to invite professionals for a meeting or workshop could change the scene. It is advisable to choose service providers in the city where the event will hold. For instance, if you settle for Paris, all your service providers will likely come from there too. It's cheaper this way and it's easy to estimate a budget after choosing the preferred region.
We believe that France is one of the best destinations in the world. The diversity of cities, landscapes, history and gastronomy are strong motto for a majority of our clients.
3. WНАТ ЅТYLЕ ОF VЕΝUЕ?
France is a multicultural country so selecting a venue style for an event is quite easy. France offers a range of modern, traditional, and even trendy event styles. Therefore, the event planner should reach out to vendors and suppliers as soon as he books the venue. Arranging a kick-off meeting with these people will prevent last minute effort duplications.
Also, last minute arrangements can cost extra if the vendors are forced to adjust their schedules to accommodate your needs. It is imperative to choose an event style that matches your budget. You should not opt for a very high style which you can't afford. Also, ensure that everything is available for the event dates. Once you make the decision about the style, it will be easy for you to draw its budget.
4. WНАТ РЕRІОD ОF ТНЕ YЕАR DО YОU РLАΝ ТНЕ ЕVЕΝТ?
The period of the year is another crucial factor to put into considering while estimating the budget for an event in France. France has warm and cold seasons, and the event manager can advise which period is suitable for a particular event.
If you are planning an event in Paris, it's better to do it between June and October. The reason is that this period is relatively warm and your guests will be comfortable. Also, summer activities are quite different from winter activities. Therefore different seasons will probably have different costs.
Each period has his key features. The local Dmc is your best adviser to select the appropriate destination regarding the period of the year.
5. НОW LОΝG ЅНОULD ТНЕ ЕVЕΝТ ВЕ?
The duration of the event is a crucial factor to consider when planning an event in France. The length of the event will help the event organizer to better plan for the event. Knowledge of the duration will also guide you in drawing a practical budget for the event.
It is advisable for the French DMC to check out affordable venues and adjust dates to minimize cost. More so, the event planner can play with the event date. For example, you could start the conference in the afternoon so that you don't spend on breakfast and lunch.
6. DО YОU RЕQUІRЕ АССОМОDАТІОΝ?
Accommodation is another important consideration to make when drawing a budget for an event in France. You need to decide whether or not you will provide accommodation for your guests. You might want to minimize cost by opting for twin bedrooms in the hotel.
The advantage is that two attendees get to share a single room. Whatever the case, just have in mind that many hotels in France offer very competitive and attractive group rates.
The majority of corporates go for 4 star properties.
You will find a larger choice and the best deals for groups in this category.
7. НОW МАΝY РЕОРLЕ ТО ВUDGЕТ?
Knowledge of the number of people you are expecting will help you come up with a reasonable budget. If you are expecting many people, you can try to negotiate a package price for certain services. For instance, when it comes to transporting your guests, you may ask to be billed per bus instead of per person.
You might also opt for vans instead of buses. Such attitudes could help you reduce expenditures. The event planner should also be able to compare the costs per person and the expenditures per groups of attendees.
8. DО YОU REQUIRE А ЅІТЕ VІЅІТ?
А sіtе іnsресtіоn іs thе bеst wау tо mаkе уоur fіnаl dесіsіоn.
The visits are organized by the local dmc to potential event venues.
The purpose of the visits is to check if the hotel and venues facilities are up to the standards for your corporate event.
Usuаllу, thе sіtе іnsресtіоn tаkеs оnе оr twо dауs. Vеndоrs аррlу dіsсоunt rаtеs for accommodation and transportation if you stay longer.
9. НОW СRЕАТІVЕ АΝD ТАІLОR МАDЕ ЅНОULD ТНЕ ЕVЕΝТ ВЕ?
Wе knоw thаt уоu wаnt thе bеst еvеnt оut thеrе, but thе rеаlіtу іs thаt nоt еvеrуоnе іs ехресtіng а mаstеrріесе. Ѕоmеtіmеs thе еvеnt јust hаs tо wоrk.
Hence, the French DMC may bring suggestions that will cause thrills and excitements. But you should have in mind that such creative ideas always increase the overall cost.
We know that you want the best event out there, but the reality is that not everyone is expecting a masterpiece.

original text:
"If you observe the onset of sudden dog stomach pain, and see symptoms such as s painful, tense abdomen it could indicate a condition that is life threatening. The abdomen can be in pain, distended, inflamed, or there could be an insufficient supply of blood to the abdomen (called ischemia). Older dogs have a higher frequency of problems such as a malignant tumor (cancerous) while young dogs could have an injury, dietary problem, infection or intestine problems.
If you only see diarrhea with no changes in the abdomen, this is usually self limiting and will resolve on its own and is usually not life threatening. The exception is diarrhea that contains a significant amount of blood. If there is just a bit of blood, the problem could be colitis from stress and is not as serious as blood red diarrhea. If you see vomiting and diarrhea, or vomiting more than 2 to 3 times in a row, then consult with a veterinarian."
Gaseous distention of a significant portion of the small intestine. However the distension is not considered sufficient to warrant a diagnosis of intestinal obstruction. One loop of bowel is more distended than the others and partial obstruction should be considered. There is a large amount of material in the stomach.
Two breeds are at higher risk for dog stomach pain.
Dalmatians: Males are at higher risk for a urethral obstruction. This is caused by higher incidences of urate urinary calculi (urinary stones).
German Shepherds: Dogs diagnosed with pancreatic atrophy have a heightened risk of a condition called mesenteric volvulus (twist in the fold that attaches the small intestine to the abdominal all). The twist causes an intestinal obstruction or blockage.
Dogs on corticosteroids and other non-steroidal anti-inflammatories are at risk for gastrointestinal ulcers perforations.
Given the many possible causes for a dog stomach pain, the veterinarian will seek to eliminate those that are most common. To do this a series of tests are conducted such as blood tests (CBC) and urine test (urinalysis).
Test results could indicate the underlying cause. For example a low number of white blood cells indicate inflammation and an infection. A gastrointestinal ulcer is detected by low red blood cell counts.
X-Rays can find any masses or abnormality in organ shape. It can also detect fluid accumulation. High gas levels could indicate an infection or a ruptured gastrointestinal tract. An Ultrasound test is used to find any abdominal masses, fluids, cysts, abscesses or urinary stones.
Testing abdominal fluid using a test called amniocentesis is used on dogs with sudden abdominal pain.
Foreign bodies can sometimes be found by palpitating the abdomen (touching the abdomen from outside the body).
If the cause cannot be determine with testing, a veterinarian may want to explore the abdomen surgically.
A specific diagnosis of the underlying cause is needed before treatment can begin. Until the cause is determined, the Vet will address any symptoms such as pain relief (analgesics). Gas production can be reduce with histamine H2 antagonists.
The Vet will also determine if emergency intervention or surgery is needed.
For symptoms such as vomiting, nothing by mouth is recommended until the underlying cause is determined. If there is fluid loss, intravenous therapy is needed.
